title: Capsule build+inject integration test on a representative microtask context
goal: End-to-end integration test that drives the full CapsuleBuilder + CapsuleInjector + CapsuleRecorder + IPC path against a representative microtask context (KernelBuilderMtImplementation task type, fixture FEMS payload, fixture ModelRuntime) to prove the D.1 primitives compose correctly before D.2 (self-improvement loop) consumes them.
expected_diff_shape: New integration test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/memory_capsule_e2e_tests.rs (no new modules). Adds tests/fixtures/memory_capsule_e2e/sample_fems_items.json with 30 fixture items mirroring the HBR test-packet corpus shape so the same fixtures can be reused by the D.2 self-improvement-loop tests.
implementation_notes: Per refinement D.1 'Tests on a representative microtask context.' Fixture: 30 FEMS items shaped like the actual HBR test-packet corpus (mix of HBR rule IDs, validator scratchpad fragments, ModelManual snippets). Test flow: (1) construct CapsuleBuilder with TestFemsAdapter loaded from fixture, (2) build a capsule for KernelBuilderMtImplementation task type with a query like 'how do I add a new HBR rule applicability tag', (3) assert capsule contains at most top_k items, all within capsule_budget_bytes, with audit_log entries for both included and excluded items, (4) construct CapsuleInjector and inject for a mock ModelRuntime call context, assert Inject decision and exactly one FR-EVT-CAPSULE-INJECTED, (5) record via CapsuleRecorder against mock KernelActionCatalogV1, assert RecordReceipt round-trips through the catalog, (6) invoke list_recent and get IPC commands against the recorded capsule, assert shape, (7) invoke suppress_item against one included item, assert FR-EVT-CAPSULE-SUPPRESSED and that subsequent get returns updated audit_log with that entry suppressed. Run with cargo test --release for realistic perf signal. Assertion includes that the full e2e completes in <500ms on stock dev hardware (NOT a hard latency gate; logs warning if exceeded).
risk_if_missed: Without end-to-end coverage the D.1 primitives may compose incorrectly at the seams (builder + injector + recorder + IPC) and D.2 will inherit broken plumbing.
validator_focus: Test exercises every D.1 surface in one run; fixture is reusable by D.2; perf signal logged but not hard-gated.

lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator review found the implementation pattern (surface-shape + trait abstraction + LiveXxxUnavailable / placeholder props) satisfied test contracts but did not satisfy what the Master Spec actually says these things do. None of the 27 MTs listed below are correct implementations. All 27 are reopened and must be redone before WP-KERNEL-004 can be considered complete. No other MT may be claimed until these 27 are correctly implemented and re-validated. MT-148 work was rolled back; it is to be revisited after these 27 close correctly.
lifecycle.deflection_summary: Same as MT-141. Capsule build+inject integration test committed (memory_capsule_e2e_tests). The 'representative microtask' fixture is hand-authored and the operator has not confirmed it actually exercises the spec's required path end-to-end.
lifecycle.wp_hard_fail_note: WP-KERNEL-004 cannot complete with these 27 MTs in NEEDS_REIMPLEMENTATION status. Each MT below is individually hard-failed AND counts as a hard-fail blocker on the WP itself. The committed code from the prior session remains on the feat branch as a base to either deepen or replace; the lifecycle status below is the source of truth for whether the MT is done, not the presence of source files.

minimum_controls: E2E test runs in CI without external dependencies (no live FEMS or ModelRuntime).
Fixture corpus is committed and shared with D.2 tests for traceability.
Perf signal is logged (not hard-gated) so D.2 has a baseline before optimizing.
